commands.review_summary:
The PR threads a replace-mode flag through `openclaw models list` and skips authenticated catalog rows in the default replace-mode view while preserving `--all` and provider-filtered catalog browsing.

PR surface: Source +9. Total +9 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main's default `models list` path calls `appendAuthenticatedCatalogRows` without checking `models.mode`, and that helper appends authenticated provider catalog rows. I did not run a live CLI reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor or maintainer follow-up is needed for real behavior proof and compatibility-sensitive coverage; the remaining blocker is not a safe automated repair because contributor setup proof must be supplied or maintainer-run.

target.title: fix(models): respect models.mode=replace in 'openclaw models list' output
target.body:
## Summary

When `models.mode: "replace"` is set with a restricted `models.providers` list, `openclaw models list` still shows models from built-in provider catalogs and authenticated-but-unconfigured providers.

## Changes

- `src/commands/models/list.list-command.ts`: Added `modelsModeReplace` flag; fixed `enableSourcePlanCascade` to not be blocked by replace mode, so `--all` and `--provider` browse work regardless of `models.mode`
- `src/commands/models/list.rows.ts`: `appendAuthenticatedCatalogRows` skips processing when in replace mode

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** When `models.mode: "replace"` is set, `openclaw models list` no longer shows models from built-in provider catalogs or authenticated-but-unconfigured providers. Explicit `--all` and `--provider <id>` browsing continues to work in replace mode, matching the documented CLI contract.

**Code change rationale:** The initial PR incorrectly blocked `enableSourcePlanCascade` with `!modelsModeReplace`, which prevented `--all` and provider-filtered catalog browsing. The replace-mode restriction is now scoped to `appendAuthenticatedCatalogRows` only.

**Proof script:** Standalone logic test covering all 8 scenarios (old vs new behavior):

```
[proof] enableSourcePlanCascade — old vs new logic

  -- Case 1: default `models list` with models.mode=replace --
  PASS  OLD: cascade=false  (correct — default list in replace mode should keep default-only)
  PASS  NEW: cascade=false  (correct — same behavior, no regression)

  -- Case 2: default `models list` with normal mode --
  PASS  OLD: cascade=false  (correct — default list stays cheap in normal mode too)
  PASS  NEW: cascade=false  (correct — no regression)

  -- Case 3: `models list --all` with models.mode=replace --
  PASS  OLD: cascade=false  (BUG — --all blocked by replace mode!)
  PASS  NEW: cascade=true   (FIXED — --all works regardless of replace mode)

  -- Case 4: `models list --provider openai` with models.mode=replace --
  PASS  OLD: cascade=false  (BUG — provider filter blocked by replace mode!)
  PASS  NEW: cascade=true   (FIXED — provider filter works regardless of replace mode)

  -- Case 5: `models list --all` with normal mode --
  PASS  OLD: cascade=true   (correct)
  PASS  NEW: cascade=true   (correct — no regression)

  -- Case 6: `models list --provider openai` with normal mode --
  PASS  OLD: cascade=true   (correct)
  PASS  NEW: cascade=true   (correct — no regression)

[proof] appendAuthenticatedCatalogRows — replace mode guard

  -- Case 7: appendAuthenticatedCatalogRows with models.mode=replace --
  PASS  skip=true  (correct — replace mode skips authenticated catalog rows)

  -- Case 8: appendAuthenticatedCatalogRows with normal mode --
  PASS  skip=false (correct — normal mode includes authenticated catalog rows)

[proof] 14 tests: 14 PASS / 0 FAIL
[proof] ALL PASS
```

**What was not tested:** Not tested against a live OpenClaw gateway with `models.mode` configured. The change is a straightforward one-line scope narrowing of the existing replace-mode guard.

Closes #94705
